title: Electrosurgical system
abstract: An electrosurgical system includes a radiofrequency (RF) generator having a controller configured to detect an occurrence of a vaporization point of target tissue and a vaporization duration between commencement of delivery of the RF energy and the occurrence of the vaporization point. The controller can apply the vaporization duration to adjust a parameter during subsequent electrode activation, such as RF power level, an electrode activation sequence, a distance between activated electrodes, and a number of activated electrodes. The controller can apply the vaporization duration to determine if whether a predetermined depth of effect has been reached for use in subsequent parameter adjustments.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An electrosurgical device, comprising: an elongate body having a distal end and a proximal end, the elongate body including a plurality of conductive signal lines; and an expandable member coupled to the distal end of the elongate body, the expandable member comprising: a non-conductive and expandable substrate having an outer surface and an inner surface to receive a fluid, the substrate having a deflated delivery configuration and an inflated expanded configuration; and a plurality of electrode pairs attached to the outer surface of the substrate and electrically coupled to the conductive signal lines, wherein each electrode pair includes an active electrode spaced-apart on the substrate from a return electrode bipolar pair; wherein the substrate includes a plurality of apertures on the outer surface disposed between the electrode pairs to expel the fluid in the expanded configuration. 2. The electrosurgical device of claim 1 wherein the proximal end is coupled to a handle. 3. The electrosurgical device of claim 2 wherein the handle includes actuatable controls to selectively apply energy to the plurality of electrode pairs and to cause expansion and deflation of the expandable member. 4. The electrosurgical device of claim 1 wherein the expandable member includes a balloon. 5. The electrosurgical device of claim 4 wherein the balloon is expanded with a gas. 6. The electrosurgical device of claim 1 wherein the electrosurgical device is a catheter assembly. 7. An electrosurgical catheter assembly, comprising: an elongate body having a distal end and a proximal end, the elongate body including a plurality of conductive signal lines; and an expandable member coupled to the distal end of the elongate body, the expandable member comprising: a non-conductive and expandable substrate having an outer surface and an inner surface to receive a fluid, the substrate having a deflated delivery configuration and an inflated expanded configuration; and a plurality of electrode pairs attached to the outer surface of the substrate and electrically coupled to the conductive signal lines, wherein each electrode pair includes an active electrode spaced-apart on the substrate from a return electrode bipolar pair; wherein in each of the electrode pairs the active electrode includes a plurality of spaced-apart active traces electrically in electrical communication, the return electrode includes a plurality of spaced-apart return traces, and the active traces are interleaved with the return traces. 8. The electrosurgical catheter assembly of claim 7 wherein the conductive signal lines are electrically couplable to a controller. 9. The electrosurgical catheter assembly of claim 8 wherein the active electrodes and return electrodes are configured to act as a single pole at the controller. 10. The electrosurgical catheter assembly of claim 7 wherein the electrode pairs include a conductive wire adhered to the outer surface. 11. A catheter assembly, comprising: an elongate body having a distal end and a proximal end, the elongate body including a plurality of conductive signal lines; and an expandable member coupled to the distal end of the elongate body, the expandable member comprising: 

 a non-conductive and compliant substrate having an outer surface and an inner surface to receive a fluid, the substrate having a deflated delivery configuration and an inflated expanded configuration; and a plurality of electrode pairs attached to the outer surface of the substrate and electrically coupled to the conductive signal lines, wherein each electrode pair includes an active electrode spaced-apart on the substrate from a return electrode bipolar pair; wherein the substrate includes a plurality of apertures on the outer surface and opened to expel the fluid in the expanded configuration and wherein the apertures are closed in the delivery configuration. 12. The catheter assembly of claim 11 wherein the substrate includes a thickness, wherein the thickness is thinned in the expanded configuration such that the apertures are opened in the substrate. 13. The catheter assembly of claim 12 wherein the apertures are opened when the substrate is under stress from expansion. 14. The catheter assembly of claim 13 wherein the apertures are closed when the substrate is released from stress. 15. An electrosurgical generator, the electro surgical generator couplable to a plurality of electrodes on an electrosurgical device to deliver a radiofrequency (RF) signal to a target tissue, the electrosurgical generator comprising: an impedance detector to generate impedance measurements indicative of an impedance associated with the target tissue; a controller responsive to the impedance measurements from the impedance detector to detect an occurrence of a vaporization point of the target tissue, detect a vaporization duration indicative of a time duration between commencement of delivery of the RF signal and the occurrence of the vaporization point, and determine if a predetermined depth of effect has been reached based at least in part on the vaporization duration; and an RF output stage to generate the RF signal in response to the controller. 16. The electrosurgical generator of claim 15, wherein the controller is configured to detect the occurrence of the vaporization point of the target tissue by detecting an increase in the impedance measurement followed by a plateau in the impedance measurement. 17. The electrosurgical generator of claim 15, comprising a depth of effect look up table having a plurality of depth of effect values, each depth of effect value associated with a vaporization duration and a power level of the RF signal, wherein the controller applies the look up table to determine if the predetermined depth of effect has been reached. 18. The electrosurgical generator of claim 17, wherein each of the plurality of depth of effect values in the depth of effect look up table is further associated with at least one of a distance between activated electrodes and a surface area associated with the plurality of electrodes of the electrosurgical device. 19. The electrosurgical generator of claim 18, wherein the controller is configured to adjust a power level for the RF signal if the predetermined depth of effect has not been reached. 20. The electrosurgical generator of claim 19, comprising a power look up table having a plurality of power adjustment values, wherein each of the power adjustment value is associated with a vaporization duration, a power level of the RF signal, and a difference between a calculated depth of effect and a target depth of effect, wherein controller applies the power look up table to determine a new power level for the RF signal. 21. The electrosurgical generator of claim 20, wherein each of the plurality of depth of effect values in the depth of effect look up table is further associated with at least one of a distance between activated electrodes and a surface area associated with the plurality of electrodes. 22. The electrosurgical generator of claim 15, wherein the impedance detector is couplable to the plurality of electrodes and the impedance measurements are indicative of the impedance between the electrodes. 23. An electrosurgical generator, the electrosurgical generator couplable to a plurality of electrodes on an electrosurgical device to deliver a radiofrequency (RF) signal to a target tissue, the electrosurgical generator comprising: an impedance detector to generate impedance measurements indicative of an impedance associated with the target tissue; a controller responsive to the impedance measurements from the impedance detector to detect an occurrence of a vaporization point of the target tissue, detect a vaporization duration indicative of a time duration between commencement of delivery of the RF signal and the occurrence of the vaporization point, and adjust a parameter of the electrosurgical generator during a subsequent activation of an electrode of the plurality of electrodes based at least in part on the vaporization duration; and an RF output stage to generate the RF signal in response to the controller. 24. The electrosurgical generator of claim 23 wherein the one or more parameter of the electrosurgical generator comprises a power level of the RF signal, a sequence of activation of the plurality of electrodes, a distance between activated ones of the plurality of electrodes, and a number of activated electrodes. 25. A radiofrequency (RF) generator for use with an electrosurgical system including a catheter assembly having a plurality of electrodes supported by the catheter assembly and configured to deliver an RF signal to target tissue, the RF generator comprising: an impedance detector configured to generate impedance measurements indicative of an impedance associated with the target tissue; a controller responsive to the impedance measurements from the impedance detector and configured to (a) detect an occurrence of a vaporization point of the target tissue, (b) detect a vaporization duration indicative of a time duration between commencement of delivery of the RF signal and the occurrence of the vaporization point, and (c) determine if a predetermined depth of effect has been reached based at least in part on the vaporization duration; and an RF output stage configured to generate the RF signal in response to the controller. 26. The RF generator of claim 25, wherein the controller is configured to modify a number of activated electrodes if it is determined that the predetermined depth of effect has not been reached. 27. The RF generator of claim 25, wherein the plurality of electrodes comprises a plurality of pairs of electrodes. 28. The RF generator of claim 27, wherein the controller is configured to activate the plurality of pairs of electrodes according to a sequence and to control the sequence of activations of the plurality of electrode pairs based on the vaporization duration. 29. The RF generator of claim 28, wherein the controller is configured to control the sequence of activations of the plurality of electrode pairs by changing a distance between electrodes comprising the electrode pairs.
